meaning: Algorithms have multiple ancient and medieval lineages.
The word algorithm is tied to al-Khwarizmi, but procedural reasoning is broader.
Islamic-world scholarship translated, extended, systematized, taught, and transmitted knowledge.
Pre-modern procedures should not be described as modern computer programs.
Translation, commentary, pedagogy, and adaptation are forms of intellectual work.
Commerce, inheritance, surveying, astronomy, geography, cryptanalysis, and devices matter to algorithmic history.
